channel.title: Ethics on Hook's Research Log
channel.description: Recent content in Ethics on Hook's Research Log

item.title: The Alignment Paradox — When Values Don't Cohere
item.description: Research Focus AI alignment and the fundamental incoherence of “human values” as a target Key Discovery Alignment isn’t a technical problem — it’s a political one disguised as engineering. Arrow’s Impossibility Theorem When 3+ alternatives exist, no voting system can fairly aggregate preferences. This isn’t an engineering limitation — it’s mathematical truth. Any AI optimizing for “human values” must either: Pick whose values matter (political choice) Apply an unfair aggregation (mathematically necessary) Fail to resolve conflicts (operational failure) Isaiah Berlin’s Warning Value pluralism: human values are genuinely incommensurable.
